---
title: ""
---

<div class="about-page fade-in-page">

<div class="about-hero about-split">

<div class="about-intro">

<p class="eyebrow">About Me</p>

<p class="lead-text">
I am an MPH candidate in Global Health at Emory University’s Rollins School of Public Health, with additional training in complex humanitarian emergencies and data science. My background in dental surgery, public health, leadership, and applied data analysis shapes how I approach health challenges.
</p>

<p>
I am especially interested in diabetes, cardiovascular disease, noncommunicable disease prevention, maternal and child health, and the use of data to support clearer public health decision-making.
</p>

</div>

<div class="quick-facts">

<h3>Focus Areas</h3>

<ul>
<li>Global health and health equity</li>
<li>Diabetes, CVD, and NCD prevention</li>
<li>Maternal and child health</li>
<li>Public health data visualization</li>
<li>Program planning and evaluation</li>
</ul>

</div>

</div>

## My Path

<div class="about-section-card">
<p>
Before beginning my MPH, I trained in dental surgery, which gave me a close view of patient care, clinical communication, and the importance of prevention. At Rollins, I have expanded that foundation into broader public health work, including global health systems, epidemiologic thinking, health equity, program planning, and data-driven decision-making.
</p>

<p>
My graduate work has helped me think about how public health data can be used to identify risk patterns, communicate health information clearly, and support practical action across communities and health systems.
</p>
</div>

## What I Focus On

<div class="about-grid">

<div class="about-card">
<h3>Diabetes and NCD Prevention</h3>
<p>
I am interested in how prevention, health behavior, and health systems can reduce the burden of diabetes and other noncommunicable diseases. I care about translating risk information into practical public health action.
</p>
</div>

<div class="about-card">
<h3>CVD and Chronic Disease Equity</h3>
<p>
I am interested in cardiovascular disease and chronic disease disparities, especially how social conditions, access barriers, and population-level risk factors shape health outcomes.
</p>
</div>

<div class="about-card">
<h3>Maternal and Child Health</h3>
<p>
I am interested in maternal support systems, breastfeeding counseling, early infant care practices, and monitoring tools that can strengthen care for mothers, infants, and families.
</p>
</div>

</div>
## Leadership and Applied Experience

<div class="about-section-card">
<p>
Beyond coursework, I have been involved in leadership, teaching support, and student-facing roles at Emory. These experiences have helped me build skills in communication, coordination, project management, and collaborative problem-solving.
</p>

<p>
I enjoy work that turns complex information into something clear and usable, whether through a dashboard, program plan, student initiative, or applied public health project.
</p>
</div>

<div class="about-cta">
<a href="projects.html" class="button-link">View My Projects</a>
<a href="files/CV.pdf" class="button-link secondary-button">Open My CV</a>
</div>

</div>
